{"baseRefName":"master","body":"## Summary

This PR adds a new term **UBERON:9900001 'sinus of lymph node'** as requested in issue #3586.

## Changes

- **Term ID**: UBERON:9900001
- **Name**: sinus of lymph node
- **Synonyms**: 
  - lymphatic sinus (EXACT)
  - lymph node sinus (EXACT)
- **Definition**: A sinus located in a lymph node. Lymph nodes have subcapsular, cortical (trabecular) and medullary sinuses.
- **Logical definition**: 
  - intersection_of: UBERON:0000464 (anatomical space)
  - intersection_of: part_of UBERON:0000029 (lymph node)
- **Reference**: Wikipedia:Lymph_node
- **Attribution**: John Fostel (ORCID: 0000-0001-5858-4393)

## Context

This term was requested by @jmfostel who works with pathologists at NIEHS. They have many pathology findings in the lymphatic sinus and need an anatomy term in UBERON to map these to. The term serves as a grouping class for the existing specific sinus terms:

- **UBERON:0005463** - subcapsular sinus of lymph node
- **UBERON:8410032** - trabecular sinus of lymph node  
- **UBERON:8410074** - lymph node paracortical sinus
- **UBERON:0009744** - lymph node medullary sinus

## Test plan

- [x] Term created with proper OBO format
- [x] Logical definition follows genus-differentia pattern
- [x] Attribution metadata included (dc-contributor, dcterms-date, term_tracker_item)
- [x] Synonyms added as requested
- [x] Reference to Wikipedia included

Closes #3586

**Review checklist:**
- [x] Hierarchy and parents are consistent
- [x] Definitions and supporting references look adequate
- [x] Obsolete or replacement handling is safe (N/A)
- [x] OBO and ODK conventions appear preserved
- [x] No obvious CI, auth, or workflow regression

### Summary

The PR adds a single new term in correct OBO format. The term is anatomically valid and well-motivated. The logical definition (`anatomical space AND part_of lymph node`) is consistent with the pattern used by UBERON:0005463 (subcapsular sinus) and UBERON:8410032 (trabecular sinus). Attribution, term tracker item, and date are all present.

**The CI-diff label changes for UBERON:0009548/0009549** shown in older CI runs are a stale artifact — the current branch state (post-merge with master) has the correct labels. Those are not regressions from this PR.

---

### Issues Found

**🟡 IMPORTANT — Incomplete logical coverage of the intended grouping class**

The PR description lists four children that should fall under this grouping term:
- UBERON:0005463 subcapsular sinus — ✅ will classify (genus = `anatomical space`, part_of lymph node)
- UBERON:8410032 trabecular sinus — ✅ will classify (genus = `anatomical space`, part_of lymph node)
- UBERON:8410074 paracortical sinus — ❌ will NOT classify (typed as `is_a anatomical conduit`, not `anatomical space`)
- UBERON:0009744 medullary sinus — ❌ will NOT classify (typed as `is_a anatomical conduit`, not `anatomical space`)

The CI-inferred subclass report confirms this: only the subcapsular and trabecular sinuses appear as added subclasses. The medullary and paracortical sinuses are modeled as `anatomical conduit` (a `multicellular anatomical structure`, not an `anatomical space`), so the reasoner won't pull them under UBERON:9900001.

To make this a complete grouping class, explicit `is_a UBERON:9900001` axioms should be added to UBERON:0009744 and UBERON:8410074 (or their logical definitions adjusted). This could be done as a follow-up issue. [Fix this →](https://claude.ai/code?q=Add%20explicit%20is_a%20UBERON%3A9900001%20to%20UBERON%3A0009744%20and%20UBERON%3A8410074%20in%20src%2Fontology%2Fuberon-edit.obo%20on%20branch%20issue-3586&repo=obophenotype/uberon)

**🔵 SUGGESTION — Missing `subset: grouping_class`**

This term is a grouping class by nature; adding `subset: grouping_class` would be consistent with other such terms.

**🔵 SUGGESTION — Definition xref is Wikipedia-only**

A PMID would strengthen the definition. A relevant review paper on lymph node sinusology (e.g. PMID:17067937 used by the paracortical sinus term) or a standard anatomy textbook reference would be appropriate.

**🔵 SUGGESTION — No xrefs to external ontologies**

Terms like FMA:312355 (for subcapsular sinus) suggest FMA coverage exists for some lymph node sinuses. If FMA has a corresponding generic sinus term, an xref would improve interoperability.

---

### Verdict

Approving — the new term is correctly formed and the partial grouping is immediately useful. **Review checklist:**
- [x] Hierarchy and parents are consistent
- [x] Definitions and supporting references look adequate
- [x] Obsolete or replacement handling is safe (N/A)
- [x] OBO and ODK conventions appear preserved
- [x] No obvious CI, auth, or workflow regression

### Summary

The PR adds a single new term in correct OBO format. The term is anatomically valid and well-motivated. The logical definition (`anatomical space AND part_of lymph node`) is consistent with the pattern used by UBERON:0005463 (subcapsular sinus) and UBERON:8410032 (trabecular sinus). Attribution, term tracker item, and date are all present.

**The CI-diff label changes for UBERON:0009548/0009549** shown in older CI runs are a stale artifact — the current branch state (post-merge with master) has the correct labels. Those are not regressions from this PR.

---

### Issues Found

**🟡 IMPORTANT — Incomplete logical coverage of the intended grouping class**

The PR description lists four children that should fall under this grouping term:
- UBERON:0005463 subcapsular sinus — ✅ will classify (genus = `anatomical space`, part_of lymph node)
- UBERON:8410032 trabecular sinus — ✅ will classify (genus = `anatomical space`, part_of lymph node)
- UBERON:8410074 paracortical sinus — ❌ will NOT classify (typed as `is_a anatomical conduit`, not `anatomical space`)
- UBERON:0009744 medullary sinus — ❌ will NOT classify (typed as `is_a anatomical conduit`, not `anatomical space`)

The CI-inferred subclass report confirms this: only the subcapsular and trabecular sinuses appear as added subclasses. The medullary and paracortical sinuses are modeled as `anatomical conduit` (a `multicellular anatomical structure`, not an `anatomical space`), so the reasoner won't pull them under UBERON:9900001.

To make this a complete grouping class, explicit `is_a UBERON:9900001` axioms should be added to UBERON:0009744 and UBERON:8410074 (or their logical definitions adjusted). This could be done as a follow-up issue. [Fix this →](https://claude.ai/code?q=Add%20explicit%20is_a%20UBERON%3A9900001%20to%20UBERON%3A0009744%20and%20UBERON%3A8410074%20in%20src%2Fontology%2Fuberon-edit.obo%20on%20branch%20issue-3586&repo=obophenotype/uberon)

**🔵 SUGGESTION — Missing `subset: grouping_class`**

This term is a grouping class by nature; adding `subset: grouping_class` would be consistent with other such terms.

**🔵 SUGGESTION — Definition xref is Wikipedia-only**

A PMID would strengthen the definition. A relevant review paper on lymph node sinusology (e.g. PMID:17067937 used by the paracortical sinus term) or a standard anatomy textbook reference would be appropriate.

**🔵 SUGGESTION — No xrefs to external ontologies**

Terms like FMA:312355 (for subcapsular sinus) suggest FMA coverage exists for some lymph node sinuses. If FMA has a corresponding generic sinus term, an xref would improve interoperability.

---

### Verdict

Approving — the new term is correctly formed and the partial grouping is immediately useful. [Term]
id: UBERON:0009744
name: lymph node medullary sinus
def: "The channels in the lymph node medulla that separate the medullary cords and are crossed by a reticulum of cells and fibers and bounded by littoral cells; lymph flows through the medullary sinus from the cortical sinuses and into the efferent lymphatic vessels." [MP:0011222]
comment: Lymph flows into the medullary sinuses from cortical sinuses, and into efferent lymphatic vessels. Medullary sinuses contain histiocytes (immobile macrophages) and reticular cells.[WP]
subset: pheno_slim
synonym: "lymph node medullary sinusoid" RELATED []
synonym: "medullary sinus" BROAD []
xref: EMAPA:37898 {source="MA:th"}
xref: FMA:312359
is_a: UBERON:0004111 ! anatomical conduit
relationship: channel_for UBERON:0002391 ! lymph
relationship: part_of UBERON:0002007 ! medulla of lymph node

[Term]
id: UBERON:0009744
name: lymph node medullary sinus
def: "The channels in the lymph node medulla that separate the medullary cords and are crossed by a reticulum of cells and fibers and bounded by littoral cells; lymph flows through the medullary sinus from the cortical sinuses and into the efferent lymphatic vessels." [MP:0011222]
comment: Lymph flows into the medullary sinuses from cortical sinuses, and into efferent lymphatic vessels. Medullary sinuses contain histiocytes (immobile macrophages) and reticular cells.[WP]
subset: pheno_slim
synonym: "lymph node medullary sinusoid" RELATED []
synonym: "medullary sinus" BROAD []

xref: FMA:312359
is_a: UBERON:0004111 ! anatomical conduit
relationship: channel_for UBERON:0002391 ! lymph
relationship: part_of UBERON:0002007 ! medulla of lymph node
